preserveUserBrands: assets/brands/* except assets/brands/example/ (example is updatable, user brands are not)

featureFlags._doc: Runtime feature flags read at Electron startup. Do NOT rely on these for security-critical defaults — a stale version.json on an offline client stays at the shipped values. Security-critical changes must land as code pushes with a version bump.

notes: v1.19.1: Captions MCP tool. Adds mcp__merlin__captions for burning Hormozi-style word-level captions onto an existing video file using the bundled ffmpeg + whisper-cli + ggml-small.en-q5_1 toolchain that already ships in the installer (.claude/tools/, see release.yml). Pipeline: ffmpeg extracts 16k mono PCM, whisper-cli runs with -ml 1 -oj for word-level timestamps, a libass .ass subtitle file is generated with bold yellow active-word highlighting over a 3-word context bubble (prev + active + next), then ffmpeg burns it in at CRF 18 with audio passthrough. All transcription is local — no upload, no third-party service. Pre-this-tool, when a user asked the in-app agent to add captions, it confabulated ffmpeg isnt installed and offered to write Python; this is the explicit affordance. New: app/captions.js (orchestration + pure ASS generator, 29 unit tests), burn-captions IPC handler in app/main.js (next to transcribe-audio, reuses the same findTool resolution path), captions tool in app/mcp-tools.js (calls into captions.js, maps captions:<step> errors to canonical mcp-error codes with proper next_action hints), Burn captions on existing video subsection in merlin-content SKILL.md routing the agent to the right tool. No new dependencies, no new bundled binaries — just exposes the toolchain that already shipped.
whatsNew: New: mcp__merlin__captions tool — burn Hormozi-style word-level captions onto an existing video using the bundled ffmpeg + whisper-cli + small.en speech model. Local transcription, no upload, no third-party tools.
Hardened: when the in-app agent is asked to add captions to a video, it now routes to a real tool instead of confabulating ffmpeg isnt installed and offering to write Python.

Hardened: 29 new tests cover the ASS subtitle builder (centisecond rounding, libass color escaping, 3-word context bubble, brace escaping in transcript text), input validation (size cap, extension allowlist, absolute path requirement), and toolchain resolution (install-first / workspace-fallback).
